source: Gaze Glass — A Family of Mortals (2).docx
heading: 6 Priya
paragraphs: Marok's jaw dropped. He shrieked and spun in circles on a cloud, his green pupils glowing.
Master will love this.
Priya curled her fingers into a ball, and Rashid trembled under Jermaine’s arm.
“Elijah. Isn’t that his name? Isn’t that Tamara’s brother?” Sonam asked.

Rashid’s eyes dropped to the dead grass. Priya’s blood boiled. She figured out that her brother had a crush on Elijah, but she wanted him to come out when he was ready. How could Sonam unveil his sexuality so callously? Did she have no shame? How could she be so hateful?
“I—” Rashid began.
“You …what?” Sonam asked, smirking with malice. “Are gay? I don’t care, but mom—”
Priya stepped in front of Sonam to block Rashid from her sight. Sonam studied her sister’s face; Priya’s eyes had widened and her nostrils flared—they were wide enough to hold blueberries.
“How dare you?” Priya asked. She was seething with anger.
“You know mom is going to disown him when I tell her—” Sonam began to say.
Without thinking, Priya shoved her sister as hard as she could. When Sonam’s back hit the ground, air rushed out of her lungs. She laid on the dirt and coughed.
“For someone who thinks they’re so smart, you’re a heartless idiot,” Priya snapped.
“No. Not just idiotic. That was wrong on so many levels!” she added. “Do you even have a heart?”

Sonam bit her bottom lip and peeled herself away from the earth. She stood up and threw a punch, but Priya grabbed her sister’s wrist and twisted it. Sonam screamed. Then, Priya winced; her gift allowed her to feel the emotional and physical sensations of those she touched.
“That’s what it feels like. Every time you choose to be the villain!” Priya growled.
Jermaine removed his arm from Rashid’s shoulder and rubbed his back.
“Your shirt is drenched,” Jermaine said.
Rashid’s eyes were glued to a white flower.
“Yo. Man. Listen. Look at me,” Jermaine said.
Rashid raised his pink eyes and blinked, releasing tears to the flora.
“You will always be my best friend. Gay or not!”
The corners of Rashid’s lips lifted and Jermaine patted his back.
Thunder grew closer and the wind began to quicken. The sky, once pink, had become purple; and stars came out to watch the drama.
Priya deepened the twist. “This is what it feels like to hurt your own family!”
Sonam shrieked, dropped to the floor, and lost control of her balance. Jermaine frowned and pulled      Rashid away from his sisters. They guided the calf up the trail and vanished.
“If you say anything to mom or dad, or do anything to hurt Rashid again, I will make your life a living hell—actually, I’ll send you to hell myself. Do you understand?” Priya warned.
Sonam remained silent. Priya glared and dug her nails into her sister’s wrist.
“DO YOU UNDERSTAND?” Priya screamed.
“Yes!” Sonam shrieked.
Priya pulled Sonam up from the dirt by her shoulder to look at her.
“You what?” Priya said coldly.
“I won’t tell mom or dad.”
“Or what will happen?” Priya asked as wind cooled her sweaty face.
“You’ll send me to hell,” Sonam muttered. “Where you came from.”
Priya scoffed and shoved Sonam back to the ground and turned away.
“You think you’re so tough,” Sonam spat from behind.
Priya sucked her teeth and clenched her fists.
“But let me tell you something,” Sonam warned.
Priya rolled her shoulders backwards and turned away. “I don’t want to hear anything from that foul mouth of yours,” Priya said. “Not a damn thing.”

Sonam stood and dusted the dirt from her dress. “I get that I can be bitchy at times, but I would never hit you or Rashid,” Sonam said. “And look at you! Fighting all of his battles for him. Pathetic! Oh! You’re physically fighting for him?”
“I might be harsh at times,” she added. “ But I’m not a barbarian.”
Priya closed her eyes and focused her awareness between her eyebrows to center herself.
“You won’t always be there to fight his battles! Life is going to mess him up!” Sonam declared.
And she wonders why boys don’t like her, Priya thought.
“You owe Rashid an apology,” Priya said. “A real one.”

Priya sighed and left her sister behind. She jumped between large, jagged stones, avoiding piles of smelly cow manure, and hurried up the trail until she found Jermaine, Rashid, and the calf. Rashid was bent over his knees. He vomited and cried. Jermaine rubbed his back and comforted him.
“Can you give us a moment?” Priya asked. “Please?”
Jermaine nodded and stepped away. He continued up the hill with the calf. Priya knelt and waited patiently. She licked her lips and searched her head for the right words.
“How can I help? What do you need?” Priya asked.
Rashid held his stomach and cried.
“She’s going to tell mom and dad and they’ll disown me,” he sobbed.
“Don’t worry. Sonam isn’t going to open her dumb mouth.”
“How do you know?”
“I’ve reminded her there are consequences to her actions.”
Rashid cleared his throat and wiped a glob of snot from under his nose.
“I’m not cheating on Tamara,” Rashid said.
Priya tilted her head. “Do you feel comfortable talking about it?”

Rashid wiped his lips with the collar of his shirt. “Tamara told me that she liked me. I thought I liked her. Elijah would come with us to coffee shops and the movies. When Tamara would go to the bathroom, there were these moments with Elijah. I don’t know how to describe it, but I didn’t feel that way with Tamara. I really did have a crush on Tamara, but there was something with Elijah,” Rashid said.
“That couldn’t have been easy for you,” Priya said.

“It’s so confusing. I still like her,”  Rashid pulled his curls and groaned. “The three of us were supposed to watch A New World on the opening day. Tamara wasn’t feeling well and we already had tickets. Elijah and I went together.”
“Was that the night you caught me smoking weed on the terrace and I offered you some?”
“Yes,” Rashid laughed.
“So … you saw the movie with him while you were stoned?” Priya asked. “How did that go?”
“Yeah. He noticed something was off when I ordered a large popcorn, two packs of candy, a large pretzel, and a large frozen mocha,” Rashid explained.
Priya cackled. “That’s hilarious!”
“Yeah. He figured it out during the previews. I couldn’t stop laughing,”
“It sounds like the two of you were having a good time!” Priya said.

Rashid scratched his scalp. “We were but I started to think about it and then I couldn’t stop thinking about it. I got so anxious that I went to the bathroom. I guess I was there long enough that he started to worry. When he found me in the bathroom, I was splashing water on my face.”
“Why didn’t you call me? I would have rushed over and picked you up,” Priya asked.
“While you were stoned?”
“Yes. I can actually catch a taxi way easier when I’m high,” Priya smiled.
They shared a laugh as mosquitoes floated over their heads.
“What happened after he found you?” Priya asked, swatting the air.
“I threw up. I told him I was having a panic attack and why I was having a panic attack.”
“You told him you liked him?” Priya asked, jaw agape.
“No! I told him that the way I feel about him is the way I should feel about his sister!”
Priya smiled. Unlike her sister, she didn’t need to make a point and held her words.
“And?” Priya asked. “What happened next?”
“He grabbed my hand. It was so sweaty that I started panicking about that.”
Priya chuckled and clasped her hands together.
“It’s not funny! I was freaking out!” Rashid cried.
“I’m not laughing at you! You’re just such a great storyteller!”
“Oh! Thanks—uh—well—he rubbed my thumb with his thumb and breathing became easier. And there was this moment—when we locked eyes—it was—”
“Magical?” Priya asked.
“Yeah. It was magical!” Rashid exclaimed, eyes bright.
A mosquito floated above Rashid’s nose. Priya grabbed it and crushed it.
“You really don’t have to do that,” Rashid said.
“Do what?” Priya tilted her head.
“Protect me from everything!” Rashid cried. “I can stand up for myself!”

“I know you can, but I will always protect you. It’s what big sisters do,” Priya said. “You can always count on me.” She smiled and kissed her brother’s forehead and could feel his heart swell with gratitude. When he fought with their mom, she was there to soothe his sadness.
“I started to realize what was happening with Elijah. We never talked about it, but I ended up breaking things off with Tamara. She didn’t take it well until I told her the truth,” Rashid said.
“That you like guys, too?”
Rashid groaned. “Yeah.”
“How did she take that?”
“She hugged me after the shock wore off.”
“That’s awesome, Rashid!”
“Yeah! I was really relieved. I still have feelings for her—”
“But I’m guessing you didn’t tell her that you have stronger feelings for her brother?”
Rashid laughed nervously.  “No?”
“Got it. So those photos Sonam found,” Priya blurted. “When did that happen?”

Rashid licked his lips and smiled.  “Prom. There was a photobooth. We were both so stoned and I asked him if we could take a photo together. Until that point, we had only hugged,” Rashid said. “And?” Priya asked. What happened next?”

“The camera inside of the booth took three photos. We had two seconds between each photo to figure out what our poses were. We decided to take a funny photo, a serious photo, and the last one would be whatever we felt in the moment. He agreed.”
“I’m getting goosebumps,” Priya said, rubbing her hands together to generate heat.
“During the serious photo—I felt lips on my cheek and—”
“YES!” Priya screamed.
Rashid closed his eyes and inhaled slowly. Priya watched his chest rise.
“I turned my head to look at him. Our faces were so close together. I could feel his breath on my lips. When Tamara kissed me, it was nice. I thought that was normal,” Rashid said.
“You really do have feelings for her—” Priya started to say.
“I do. I thought I was going to marry that girl when we first met,” Rashid admitted.
“I hear you,” Priya assured him. “What happened with Elijah?”
Rashid grabbed his knees. “For once in my life I didn’t have any thoughts. I knew what to do. I didn’t have to think about it. I leaned in and put my lips against his.”
Priya nodded. “How did it feel?”
Rashid looked up at the full moon. It was as bright as his smile.
“Perfect,” Rashid said. “I wanted to eat his face.”
Priya wrapped her arms around her brother and gave him a squeeze. She felt his bliss.
“You are worthy of a love this great,” Priya said. “You really are!”
Rashid’s eyes watered. “I don’t know what I’d do if you weren’t here,” Rashid admitted.
Priya put her chin on her brother’s curls and sighed. “Be true to yourself and do what’s right. Fight for what you believe in,” Priya said. She was happy for him and proud to be his sister.
